entry.title: Bulk Managing SAP HCM Payroll Data
entry.content:
Business Case: Mass Updates in SAP HCM Payroll

Before choosing a tool, it is worth understanding your architecture. SAP customers running SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) integrated with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) have two fundamentally different paths for loading employee data in bulk.

Option A - SAP SuccessFactors Data Import/Export (Recommended)

The cleanest approach is to use the built-in Data Import/Export feature inside SAP SuccessFactors. Data entered here is the system of record in EC, and it replicates automatically to ECP via the standard integration. This means:

  • Data is consistent between EC and ECP at all times.
  • The employee sees their own data accurately in the self-service people profile.
  • Audit trails and approvals flow through the normal SuccessFactors workflow.

For most organizations, this is the right approach for any data  that employees can view or that HR needs to report on across the full HR suite.

Option B - Direct ECP Tools (LSMW, PA70, HRFE_MASS_UPDATE)

For data that is purely operational payroll data - managed exclusively by the Payroll Administrator, invisible to employees, and with no corresponding object in EC - loading directly into ECP via a transaction-based tool is acceptable. Common examples include:

  • Irregular income wage types (IT0015) for internal allowance schemes
  • Recurring income (IT0014) for position allowance
  • One-time statutory contributions manage by payroll only.

Note: Going direct into ECP bypasses EC entirely. The data will not appear in SuccessFactors EC, is not subject to EC workflows, and creates no EC record. Use this path only when those conditions are acceptable.

With that context established, let us look at the three main tools available for direct ECP mass update.

Tool Comparison: PA70, LSMW, and HRFE_MASS_UPDATE

SAP provides three well-known tools for bulk infotype management. Each has a specific sweet spot:

CriterionPA70 (Fast Entry)Legacy System Migration Workbench (LSMW)HRFE_MASS_UPDATE
Primary UseQuick manual entry for small groupLegacy data migration; one time large loadsRecurring operational mass updates
Volume LimitVisible Screen limit (20 records), limit 999 records per sessionNo limitNo limit
Input MethodManual, on-screen onlyFile upload with field mappingFile upload or online entry
Rollback/Undo❌None built-in❌Complex re-run required✅Instant, atomic
Background Job⚠️Limited✅Yes✅Yes
Setup complexityLowHigh (consultant required)Medium
Who runs itHR AdministratorIT/Functional ConsultantHR Administrator

 Reference: SAP Help Portal - HRFE_MASS_UPDATE

Technical prerequisite for HRFE_MASS_UPDATE: The PC_UI indicator in table T77S0 (group CCURE) must be active. This switch is set once during system setup by the Basis/Configuration team. Without it, HRFE_MASS_UPDATE will not function.

When to Use Which Tool

Use PA70 when: An HR admin needs to quickly update data for 5-20 employees and does not have time to prepare file. Ideal for truly small, one-off, manual tasks.

Use LSMW when: You are migrating historical data from a legacy system before go-live, or running a one-time annual load where the IT team owns the process. LSMW's flexibility handles scenario HRFE_MASS_UPDATE cannot, such as creating HR Actions or updating infotypes not yet supported for mass update.

Use HRFE_MASS_UPDATE when: You have a recurring operational process (monthly allowance, quarterly bonus IT0015 records, periodic allowance loads) where the HR team owns execution and you need a reliable rollback mechanism. This is the tool purpose-built for payroll operations teams.

The Real Challenge: What Happens When You Need to Undo a Batch?

Imagine this common scenario: Your Payroll Administrator loads irregular income data (IT0015) for 800 employees - Travel allowance, Meal Allowances, or any income that employees receive irregularly. These are uploaded in batch lots of 100, 200, or 1,000 records at a time.

After uploading, the team realizes that one specific batch lot was imported with the wrong amount - perhaps the wrong wage type was used, or the date period is off by one month. The question becomes: 

🤔 The key question: Can we filter the records from that specific batch lot and delete only those records - without touching any other records uploaded before or after?

With standard SAP tools used in their default configuration, the answer is: not easily. There is no built-in batch identifier on IT0015 records. You would need to manually identify each record by employee number, date, and wage type - a slow and error-prone process for large volumes.

The solution lies in a concept we call the Batch ID Mechanism - and it can be implemented entirely with standard SAP fields, no custom development required.

The Batch ID Mechanism Explained

The Batch ID Mechanism is a simple operational pattern built on two standard SAP fields that already exist on IT0015 (Additional Payments): Assignment Number and Record Sequence Number

SAP FieldRole in Batch ID Mechanism
Assignment NumberActs as the Batch ID - you populate this with a unique key (e.g. 'XX9999') shared by every record in the same upload batch.
Record Sequence NumberA system-generated key that uniquely identifies each IT0015 record. Used as the deletion key when you want to remove specific records.

How It Works - Three Step

1. Tag the batch. Generate your own Batch ID and populate the Assignment Number field - use a consistent, unique value (e.g. 'XX9999') for all records in the same upload batch. This is your batch fingerprint.

2. Find the records. After upload, use a query tool (e.g., SE16 on table PA0015, or SAP Query SQ01, SQ02 and SQ03 to generate report) to retrieve all records that share the Batch ID (Assignment Number = 'XX9999'). Note the Record Sequence Number for each record.

3. Delete the batch. Run HRFE_MASS_UPDATE with Operation = Delete, filtering by the Batch ID. All records tagged with that Batch ID are removed atomically, leaving every other batch untouched.

Visual: Before and After Batch Delete

🤓Analogy: This pattern is conceptually similar to a database transaction rollback - commit a batch, receive a token, use the token to undo. The difference is that here, you create the token yourself by populating the Assignment Number field before the upload.

Using HRFE_MASS_UPDATE: Step by Step

HRFE_MASS_UPDATE (introduced by SAP Note 3426157) is the recommended tool for the Batch ID workflow. The transaction is supported for HCM Localization infotypes (SAP Note 3474709) and all International infotypes (SAP Note 3504532).

The transaction walks through five steps.

Step 1 - Define Infotype, Subtype, and Time Period

Specify which infotype you are updating and foe which date range. 

- Infotype Version 26 applies Thailand country-specific and validations

- Leave End date blank to create open-ended records.

Step 2 - Choose Save Option

  • Save records directly - writes to the database immediately. User for smaller volume or when instant feedback is needed.
  • Create Job - schedules a background batch job. Recommended for uploads affecting 1,000+ employees to avoid dialog work process timeouts.

Best Practice: For monthly allowance uploads at scale, always use Create Job and schedule it for off-peak hours to minimise locking contention.

Step 3 - Choose Employee Selection Method

Four options are available:

Selection MethodBest For
Upload or enter together with infotype dataMost common: upload an Excel/CSV containing both personal number and field values.
Upload or manual preselectionManually type a list of personnel numbers, then enter data on-screen.
Preselection with Ad Hoc QueryRun a quick ad hoc query to dynamically select employees (e.g., all employees in a cost center).
Preselection using ReportLink a pre-built SAP Query report as the selection source - no programming required.

Step 4 - Choose Operation

OperationDescription
CreateCreates new infotype records as specified. Fails if a conflicting record already exists.
Create with proposalShows a preview of each record before committing - use this for the first run of any new template.
ChangeModifies fields in existing records. Option to restrict to locked records only.
DeleteRemoves existing records. Filter by Batch ID (Assignment Number) for targeted deletion.
Lock Locks records to prevent payroll from picking them up during review.
UnlockRelease previously locked records.

Tip: Always use Create with proposal on the first run of any new upload template. It lets you catch field-mapping errors before writing anything to the database.

Step 5 - Upload Data, Execute, and Review Results

For file based uploads, drag and drop or paste your personnel numbers and data into the entry screen. After execution, the result list shows every record processed:

Note the Assignment Number column (BatchID = XX9999). This is your rollback key for the entire batch.

Real-World Example: Monthly Allowance Load

Company processes thousand of allowance IT0015 records every month. Here is how the Batch ID pattern supports their operational workflow:

Step 1:  Select Create with Proposal to prepare Assignment Number data (or Batch ID) on screen record and maintain data  or prepare the monthly data on the Excel template from HRFE_MASS_UPDATE to upload.

By selecting Create with Proposal Option, it shows the template screen to maintain the default data for all records. 

The Assignment Number of all records is default with the value from the proposal.

On the Create screen click Export button to download Excel template. After maintain value completely, click Import button to update data on the screen and click save to create records on IT0015.

 

 

 

 

Step 2: Payroll Verification. The payroll team reviews the imported records. Because all records share the same Batch Id, any count or total discrepancy is immediately traceable to the specific batch.

 

Step 3: Correction if needed. If an error is found: provide the Batch ID -> run the query report by using Batch ID on the assignment number as a key to find the list and get the Record Sequence Number for each. In this example, I created the SAP Query report to list out the IT0015 data.

The RNo column is a required record key for the Delete operation in case employee has the record on the same date more than one. The RNo column will help define the record to delete it correctly. Run the query by using the Assignment Number as a key to filter the record for the deletion.

Export data from the report in the spreadsheet format that we can use to import it into HRFE_MASS_UPDATE for the delete operation.

 

Step 4 Run HRFE_MASS_UPDATE operation = Delete filtering by import the file with Record Sequence Number value that downloaded from the query report. --> all affected record are removed in one shot.

After import the file successfully, the data shows on the screen and ready to take the Delete action by clicking Save button. The system will show the message delete successfully, the data in IT0015 is deleted.

Summary and Quick Reference

The Batch ID Mechanism require no custom ABAP development. It is built entirely on two standard SAP fields and standard transaction operations. The key insight is:

  • Assignment Number = your self-managed Batch ID key.
  • Record Sequence Number = the system's deletion key.
  • HRFE_MASS_UPDATE Delete operation = the rollback mechanism.
  • The combination gives payroll operations a reversible, auditable bulk data process.

SAP Note Reference: 

  • 3426157 HRFE_MASS_UPDATE - new functionality and prerequisites
  • 3474709 HCM localization infotypes supported in mass update
  • 3504532 International Infotypes supported in mass update

Closing Thoughts

The Batch ID Mechanism is a simple idea, but it changes the operation for payroll administrators working with high-volume infotype data. What used to require IT intervention and careful manual searching can now be handled end-to-end by the HR team - faster, safer, and with full auditability.

entry.title: National Transfer in SAP ECP: Same PERNR vs New PERNR Decision Guide
entry.content:
The Transfer Question That Trips Up Every ECP Implementation

When an employee moves between legal entities in the same country, a single technical question drives the entire HR configuration: same Personnel Number (PERNR), or new one?

It sounds simple. It is not. The answer touches payroll continuity, data protection, statutory reporting, and a BAdI that most implementations have not yet addressed. This document walks through the decision, the SAP-confirmed technical path, and the authorization design — based on SAP Notes 3124029 and 1902797, SAP Help Portal (1H 2026), and live project experience.

Two Scenarios — One Decision

Scenario A — Same PERNR

The Job Information portlet on the existing Employee Central (EC) employment is updated to the new company. No new employment is created, no new User ID is assigned, and no new PERNR is generated in ECP. The employee's payroll history, year-to-date (YTD) results, and infotype records remain on a single personnel number.

Scenario B — New PERNR

The old employment is terminated and a new employment is created in EC. ECP terminates the old PERNR and hires a new one. YTD payroll results do not transfer. Manual entry and custom development are required.

SAP Default Behavior

SAP's default is Scenario B. Every time a new company code (BUKRS) appears in the EC-to-ECP replication, the system checks the Employee Key Mapping table (HRSFEC_D_EEKEYMP) and — if no PERNR is mapped for that company code — creates a new one. Even if the EC side keeps the same employment, ECP generates a Termination + Hiring action pair automatically.

Tax result accumulation is another key consideration. With a new employment, the YTD  tax result from previous company must be manually entered as opening balances on the new PERNR before the first payroll run - there is no standard SAP program to carry them across. With the same employment, tax result accumulation continues uninterrupted on a single record.

Figure 1: Manage Employments across the SuccessFactors suites National Transfer — Decision guide for same employment or new employment (SAP Employee Central, May 2024)

How to Achieve Same PERNR: The BAdI

Per SAP Note 3124029, same PERNR is only possible when all three of the following conditions are satisfied simultaneously:

ConditionRequirement

EC EmploymentSame employment - no new employment created
CountrySame country - cross country is not possible
Data TimingCompany change in EC is after the Full transmission Start Date (FTSD)

When all three are met, custom code is still required. BAdI HRSFEC_B_CE_DECIDE_HIRE_REHIRE must be implemented to prevent ECP from treating the company code change as a new hire. Without this BAdI, ECP creates a new PERNR regardless of the EC employment status.

Mandatory Technical Prerequisite No BAdI = No same PERNR. Implementing HRSFEC_B_CE_DECIDE_HIRE_REHIRE is the single mandatory prerequisite for Scenario A. This is a modification-free BAdI enhancement point.

The Mid-Month Wall

There is one hard constraint that no BAdI can remove: changing the payroll area in Infotype 0001 mid-payroll-period triggers RP 221 — Payroll area change possible only at the end of a payroll period. This is an ABAP kernel constraint documented in KBA 2536159 and is not configurable.

A cross-company transfer almost always changes the payroll area, since each legal entity typically has its own. The implications are clear:

  • Transfer at period boundary (month-end): Scenario A works.
  • Transfer mid-month: Scenario A is blocked. Scenario B must be used.

Practical Implication If the customer's HR policy allows transfers at any time of month, it is not possible to standardize on Scenario A for all cases. An exception process must be designed,or a business rule enforced that restricts transfers to the last day of the month.

Solving the Authorization Question

The most common objection to Scenario A same PERNR is: "The old company HR administrator will still be able to see our employee's data." This is a legitimate concern — but it is solvable without creating a new PERNR.

Figure 2: Move-date and Period of Responsibility — when an employee transfers between personal areas, the move-date marks the change of administrator responsibility (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

T582A-VALDT: The Authorization Time Logic Setting

SAP's authorization time logic, documented in SAP Note 1902797, controls access via the V_T582A field T582A-VALDT:

  • SPACE:  VALDT = SPACE: The administrator is authorized if they were responsible for the employee even one day — no time restriction applies.
  • 'X':  VALDT = 'X': Time-dependent. Access is checked against the current system date relative to the transfer date (move-date).

 

 

Figure 3: T582A-VALDT logic — the system determines whether authorization is time-dependent (VALDT = 'X') or unrestricted (VALDT = SPACE) (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

Access Matrix: VALDT = 'X' Active

Administrator

Period

Read

Write

Old company

Before move-date

Yes

Yes

Old company

After move-date

Yes (until tolerance expires)

No

Old company

After tolerance time

No

No

New company

Before move-date

Yes (historical read)

No

New company

From move-date onward

Yes

Yes

 

Figure 4: Tolerance Time (1) — old administrator retains write access until Move-date + T (T = AUTSW ADAYS in T77S0, default 15 days) (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

Figure 5: Tolerance Time (2) — after tolerance window expires, old administrator loses all access to the employee's records (SAP AG, Time Logic in HR Authorizations)

Infotype Strategy for Clean Segregation

Not every infotype requires a new record on transfer date. The following approach achieves clean data segregation:

InfotypeAction RequiredRationale
IT0008 - Basic PayCreate new record on transfer date with new company salary structureNew record falls in new admin's responsibility period only
IT0014 - Additional PaymentsCreate new record on transfer dateSensitive compensation data; old admin must not see new values
IT0006 - AddressesNo change neededBoth companies share read access during overlap - operationally acceptable
IT0009 - Bank DetailsNo change neededBoth companies originally held this data.
IT0021 - Family/Related PersonNo change neededShared demographic data; dual read access is acceptable

For custom authorization logic, BAdIs HRPAD00AUTH_CHECK and HRPAD00AUTH_TIME are available as modification-free enhancement points (available since SAP 4.6C).

 Thailand Statutory Reporting — Status

Report

Same PERNR

Notes

Personal Income Tax 91 Form

Supported

Full-year income on one PERNR; run PC00_M26_CTX91 with PERNR + form authorization

Monthly Tax Form PND1 / Annual Tax Form PND1A

Supported

PNPCE database with time-dependent logic; Tax Payer ID (Company Code) partitions the records

Social Security Report SSO 1-03, 1-03/1, 6-09

Supported

SAP Note 3749752 — handles SSO ID/branch change under same PERNR; apply before go-live

Workmen's Compensation Report

Supported

Standard PNP/PNPCE time-dependent logic; partitioned by Tax Payer ID

Decision Guide

Before committing to a design, answer these four questions in sequence:

#

Question

Answer

Direction

1

Do employees ever transfer mid-month?

Yes

Scenario B required for those cases (RP 221 constraint)

2

Is the company change on the same EC employment, in the same country?

No to either

Scenario A is impossible by design

3

Is YTD payroll continuity important?

Yes

Scenario A strongly preferred. Scenario B needs custom YTD copy program — no SAP standard tool exists

4

Is strict old-company data isolation required?

Yes

Configure T582A-VALDT='X' per infotype and create new records for sensitive infotypes (IT0008, IT0014). New PERNR is rarely justified by authorization concerns alone

Summary

Keeping the same PERNR across a national cross-company transfer is technically achievable in SAP ECP. However, it requires four elements to be in place:

#RequirementDetail
1BAdI HRSFEC_B_CE_DECIDE_HIRE_REHIRECustom implementation to block the default new-hire action triggered by a company code change
2Transfer timingPeriod boundary only (month-end). Mid-month transfers are blocked by RP 221 — this constraint is not configurable
3Authorization designT582A-VALDT='X' with selective new infotype records on transfer date for sensitive compensation data
4Thailand SSO reportsSAP Note 3749752 applied before go-live to handle SSO ID/branch change under same PERNR

When these four elements are in place, Scenario A delivers automated YTD continuity, a single PIT91 for the full tax year, and significantly lower operational workload compared to creating a new PERNR for each cross-company transfer.

Source: SAP Note 3124029 (BAdI for same PERNR, v4 Nov 2025); SAP Note 1902797 (HR Authorization period of responsibility, v7 Sep 2025); SAP Note 3749752 (SSO same PERNR); SAP Help Portal — Implementing ECP (1H 2026); KBA 2536159 (RP 221 constraint)

entry.title: Customer Returns (BKP) – Create Credit Memo Request Only After Goods Receipt
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement from business in the Customer Returns (BKP) scope item in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and I would appreciate your guidance on whether this can be achieved using standard SAP functionality/configuration.

Business Scenario

We receive empty dunnage/packaging material from the customer and process the return using the standard Customer Returns process.

Currently, when we create the Returns Order, the following are maintained:

  • Refund Control: Credit Memo Request

As a result, the Credit Memo Request is created automatically before the Goods Receipt (GR) of the returned goods is posted.

Current Process

Returns Order
→ Credit Memo Request created automatically
→ Returns Delivery
→ Goods Receipt

Required Process

Returns Order
→ Returns Delivery
→ Goods Receipt completed successfully
→ Credit Memo Request created

The business requirement is that the customer should be credited only after the returned goods have been physically received and the Goods Receipt has been successfully posted.

Important Constraint

We cannot use Refund Control = Decide Later or No Refund because, in our scenario, these options change the Requirement Type in the Procurement tab to MSR.

We need to retain the current Refund Control setting, as changing it impacts the procurement requirement type and does not meet our business requirement.

Question

Is there any standard configuration or standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that allows us to:

  • Retain Refund Control = Credit Memo Request

  • Create the Credit Memo Request only after the Goods Receipt is successfully completed

Any guidance or confirmation from experts would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: New Course: Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP IPD
entry.content:
New Self-Paced Course: Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP Integrated Product Development

Collaboration across company boundaries is essential for successful product development. The new self-paced SAP Learning course, “Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP Integrated Product Development”, helps consultants understand how SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) enables secure and efficient collaboration with suppliers and external partners throughout the product lifecycle.

In this course, you'll learn how to create collaboration workspaces, manage and share documents, track changes, collaborate on product data, and support decision-making through workflows. The course also highlights how SAP IPD helps increase productivity, improve communication, reduce errors, and ensure stakeholders always work with the latest product information. 

A great learning opportunity for SAP consultants who want to deepen their understanding of collaborative product development and supplier integration scenarios with SAP IPD. 

👉 start learning today: Collaborating with Suppliers and Business Partners with SAP Integrated Product Development

#SAPLearning #SAPIPD #PLM #ProductDevelopment #SupplyChainCollaboration #SAPCommunity

entry.title: DKS configuration
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

 

We are requesting your support to clarify the steps for integrating our BODS 4.3 development environment application, identified as DKS, into the FOCUSED RUN monitoring system.

We are requesting your support to confirm the correctness of each of the activities we will perform in the BODS 4.3 development environment, specifically on the server (IP_ADDRESS), where the application resides on Windows Server DIGITS Standard. We require your confirmation that the indicated action is correct; otherwise, please inform us what modifications or changes are necessary for its proper implementation.

 

Prerequisites

The sapadm administration account must be part of the administrators group on the Windows server.

At the operating system level, add the IPs and hostname of the server where SAP FOCUSED RUN resides to the /etc/hosts file.

This information was provided by the FOCUSED RUN administrator.

IP_ADDRESS HOST
IP_ADDRESS HOST
IP_ADDRESS HOST
IP_ADDRESS HOST

Where

Server: HOST

IP: IP_ADDRESS

Puerto: DIGITS

Usuario: NAME

 

SAP HOST AGENT

According to note DIGITS - SAP Host Agent Outside Discovery ComputerSystem configuration, the SAP Host Agent version should be SAP Host Agent 7.22 PL 69 introduces the required-local parameter and DIGITS - SAP Host Agent 7.22 PL69.

This is the same version that is installed on this server.

C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\exe\convertoscolfilter.exe: 722 (EX3), patch DIGITS, changelist DIGITS, NTAMD64, opt (EX3) (Oct 10 DIGITS, 10:07:26)

C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\exe\sapcimc.exe: 722 (EX3), patch DIGITS, changelist DIGITS, NTAMD64, opt (EX3) (Oct 10 DIGITS, 10:07:26)

**********************************************************

--------------------

SAPHOSTAGENT information

--------------------

kernel release                722

kernel make variant           722_EX3_REL

compiled on                   NT 6.2 DIGITS  x86 MS VC++ 19.29 for NTAMD64

compiled for                  64 BIT

compilation mode              Non-Unicode

compile time                  Oct 10 DIGITS 07:08:51

patch number                  69

latest change number          DIGITS

---------------------

supported environment

---------------------

operating system

Windows NT 5.1

Windows NT 5.2

Windows NT 6.0

Windows NT 6.1

Windows NT 6.2

Windows NT 6.3

Windows NT 10.0

C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\exe>hostname

HOST

C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\exe>

 

Would these be the necessary prerequisites to be able to integrate with SAP FOCUSED RUN?

 

According to note DIGITS - AC Tool release NOTE for supported products such as Data Services 4.X, BOE 4.X, BOE embedded Tomcat (Web Tier), Tomcat7, Tomcat8, it is suggested to use the AC Tool (AutoConfigure Tool) for preparing the managed system configuration of different products.

Download and unzip actool_mini.zip attached and follow the instructions in file readme.txt

Before running the run_actool.bat script, should the instructions in point 2 be followed for our SAP Data Services 4.3 application?

 

2. maintain products.xml and boe.properties and/or ds.properties

BOE:

maintain boe.properties for all empty varaibles and adapt UNIX type folders

 

DS or IS:

copy conf/products_miniboe.xml to conf/products.xml

maintain ds.properties for all varaibles <***>.

 

This part does not apply because it is a Windows operating system.

For UNIX only:

maintain boe.properties 2 varaibles (note the 2 space around '=' is required)

boe.home = <***>

jvmmond.install.path = <***>

This section must be completed for our application.

BOE Tomcat:

copy conf/products_tomcat.xml to conf/products.xml

copy boe_tomcat_mini.propertie to boe.properties

maintain boe.properties for all varaibles <***> and adapt folder for UNIX/Windows.

 

To execute the following instruction, where can we obtain the TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war file and isagent-zip-rtv-108.0.0.zip, which are not in the actool_mini folder?

 

copy TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war and isagent-zip-rtv-108.0.0.zip to folder downloads/.

If 108.0.0 is not the latest version, you need to adapt boe.properties wily.agent.version

 

See download/readme.txt on where to find above file.

download/readme.txt

For BOE Tomcat SolMan setup, copy here:

Introscope IS (isagent-zip-rtv-108.0.0.zip) from SMP

TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB (sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war) from SAP note DIGITS

 

According to note DIGITS, the file TomcatSLDDataSupplier_260624.zip was downloaded and its contents are:

C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\TomcatSLDDataSupplier_260624>dir

 El volumen de la unidad C es OSDisk

 El número de serie del volumen es: DIGITS-DIGITS

 Directorio de C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\TomcatSLDDataSupplier_260624

16/07/DIGITS  11:00 p. m.    <DIR>          .

16/07/DIGITS  11:00 p. m.    <DIR>          ..

16/07/DIGITS  11:00 p. m.         1,397,737 sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplier.war

               1 archivos      1,397,737 bytes

 

According to the Apache_Tomcat_SLD-DS_Overview.pdf document attached to note DIGITS, the SAP metadata XML file should be located in the TomcatSLDDataSupplier_260624 folder, but as we have observed before, only the .war file sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplier.war exists.

  • SLD Data Supplier can automatically discover and report deployed application to SLD. To be found by the SLD-DS application has to be delivered with a SAP “metadata” XML file which describes software component(s) implemented by the application.
  • SAP metadata XML file has to be in the META-INF folder of the application’s WAR file.
  • The structure of meta-data file reflects the PPMS model.

 

By checking the actual_mini\actool_mini\conf\ppms folder, we can find the file SAP_metadata.xml.

Is this the file referred to in the Apache_Tomcat_SLD-DS_Overview.pdf document?

 

C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\actool_mini\actool_mini\conf\ppms>dir /s

 El volumen de la unidad C es OSDisk

 El número de serie del volumen es: DIGITS-DIGITS

 Directorio de C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\actool_mini\actool_mini\conf\ppms

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          .

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          ..

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          boe_webapp

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          dswsbobje

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.            16,894 ppms_SBOPEnterpriseXI40.xml

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.               764 ppms_SBOPPAServerXI40.xml

               2 archivos         17,658 bytes

 Directorio de C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\actool_mini\actool_mini\conf\ppms\boe_webapp

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          .

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          ..

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.             1,153 SAP_metadata.xml

               1 archivos          1,153 bytes

 

 Directorio de C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\actool_mini\actool_mini\conf\ppms\dswsbobje

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          .

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.    <DIR>          ..

02/07/DIGITS  10:31 p. m.             1,064 SAP_metadata.xml

               1 archivos          1,064 bytes

     Total de archivos en la lista:

 

In the document Apache_Tomcat_SLD-DS_20250601.pdf, section 3.1, Install SLDREG – executable,

What would be the criteria for creating the .CFG and .KEY files?

 

To configure SLDREG create appropriate .CFG and .KEY files to be used to establish a connection to

the SLD. The path to the SLDREG executable and to the .CFG files have to be specified in the SLD –DS

configuration properties file like follows.

 

By the way,    Are SLDREG_PATH and SLDREG_CONFIG_FILE_PATH environment variables that must be created at the operating system level?

SLDREG_PATH=/usr/sap/hostcontrol/exe/sldreg

SLDREG_CONFIG_FILE_PATH= /tmp/sld.cfg

 

In point 3.3.1 Configuration of the SLD-DS, what parameters should we include in the sldDataSupplier.properties.template or sldDataSupplier.properties file? Initially, it would be those that appear by default.

What would be the criteria for defining the parameters for BODS 4.3?

 

In the file actol_mini\readme.txt in point 3.

C:\Users\jdominguez25\Downloads\actool_mini\actool_mini>

3. run ACTool by

Windows: run_actool.bat

Y en ACTool User Guide-2.pdf en punto 5.8

5.8 Run ACTool with Interactive User Input

For all steps, you need to run ACTool as root user or sudo on UNIX, or as the administrator role in Windows.

Windows run_actool.bat.

 

The next step is to run run_actool.bat, but upon reviewing the script, we find some instructions (in black) related to configuring and restarting Data Services. This raises the question: Did the application configuration change, requiring a restart?

Is confirmation required to determine if the configuration changed and a restart is necessary to request permission from the client or application owner?

 

@echo off

setlocal enabledelayedexpansion

if exist downloads\sap.com-TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB-*.war (

if NOT exist downloads\sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war (

              cd downloads

              rename sap.com-TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB-*.war sap.com~TomcatSLDDataSupplierWEB.war

              cd ..

)

)

reg query "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Suite XI 4.0\Installer\PERSON_NAME" /v path  |findstr path >./tmp.txt

if errorlevel 1 (

reg query "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Suite XI 4.0\Installer\LTS" /v path  |findstr path >./tmp.txt

)

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~22%>tmp.txt

set /p BOEDIR= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

rem set BOEDIR=C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects

if NOT "%BOEDIR%" == "" (

rem call cpbosdk.bat "%BOEDIR%"

set "CESDK=%BOEDIR%\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\java\lib\cesdk.jar"

)

type conf\ds.properties |findstr /b ds\.service\.name >tmp.txt

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~18%>tmp.txt

set /p DS_SERVICE_NAME= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

rem echo DS_SERVICE_NAME=%DS_SERVICE_NAME%

 

 

type conf\ds.properties |findstr /b ds\.restart >tmp.txt

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~13%>tmp.txt

set /p DS_RESTART= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

rem echo DS_RESTART='%DS_RESTART%'

 

type conf\boe.properties |findstr /b cms\.ssl >tmp.txt

set /p myvar= < tmp.txt

echo %myvar:~10%>tmp.txt

set /p CMSSSL= < tmp.txt

del tmp.txt

set BOEJVMBIN=%BOEDIR%\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\sapjvm\bin

 

if NOT exist "%BOEJVMBIN%\java.exe" (set BOEJVMBIN=C:\Windows\System32)

rem For Standalone Tomcat, uncomment below line and adapt your JDK

rem set BOEJVMBIN=C:\jdk1.7.0_25\bin

 

set BOEJVMBIN2=%BOEDIR%\Analytics\win64_x64\sapjvm\bin

echo %BOEJVMBIN2%>tmp.txt

if NOT exist "%BOEJVMBIN%\java.exe" (set /p BOEJVMBIN= < tmp.txt)

del tmp.txt

 

if NOT exist "%BOEJVMBIN%\java.exe" (echo no JVM found, please set this variable BOEJVMBIN manually! && GOTO:eof)

echo using JVM=%BOEJVMBIN%

 

set myvar=

rem for /r %%i In (AutoConfigure_lib\*.jar sap_actool*.jar) DO set myvar=!myvar!%%i;

for /r %%i In (sap_actool*.jar) DO set myvar=!myvar!%%i;

rem echo %myvar%

 

set CLASSPATH=.;%myvar%

 

rem set JAVA_OPT=-Xrunjdwp:transport=dt_socket,address=DIGITS,server=y,suspend=n

set JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT% -Dtracelog.logdir=logs -Dtracelog.configfile=BO_trace.ini -Dtracelog.name=ac

set JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT% -Djava.library.path="%BOEDIR%\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64"

rem echo JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT%

rem below for CMS SSL

if "%CMSSSL%" == "true" (

set JAVA_OPT=%JAVA_OPT% -Dbusinessobjects.orb.oci.protocol=ssl -DcertDir="%BOEDIR%\SSL" -DtrustedCert=cacert.der -DsslCert=servercert.der -DsslKey=server.key -Dpassphrase=passphrase.txt -Dpsecert=cert.pse

)

if NOT "%CESDK%" == "" (

set "CLASSPATH=%CLASSPATH%;%CESDK%"

echo Using unique BOSDK lib:"%CESDK%"

)

rem echo CLASSPATH=%CLASSPATH%

"%BOEJVMBIN%\java" %JAVA_OPT% com.sap.ac.entry.ConsoleModeLauncher %*

 

if "%DS_RESTART%" == "true" (

if NOT "%DS_SERVICE_NAME%" == "" (

sc stop %DS_SERVICE_NAME%

sc start %DS_SERVICE_NAME%

)

)

 

We need your support to address these questions or concerns so we can make the necessary changes to integrate this development environment into FOCUSED RUN's monitoring system.

 

If you believe we should change the order of the steps or activities to be performed, please also confirm, as we need to integrate the quality assurance and production environments into FOCUSED RUN's monitoring system.

Best regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content:
Just a few days after celebrating my 30-year anniversary at SAP, I had the privilege of attending my first SAP Academic Community Conference North America at the University of California, Irvine – Paul Merage School of Business.

While I shared my reflections on LinkedIn, I also wanted to take a moment to thank this incredible community directly.

To the SAP University Alliances team, the faculty members, and my SAP colleagues—thank you for welcoming me into such an inspiring network.

As someone who has spent three decades at SAP while also serving as an Adjunct Faculty Member at West Chester University of Pennsylvania, this conference brought together two passions that have shaped my career: enterprise technology and higher education.

It was an honor to present alongside Isadora Royer and Ana Luiza Treichel Vianna on the SAP Campus Ambassador Program, showcasing how our SAP colleagues can partner with universities to mentor students, collaborate with faculty, and help prepare the next generation of business and technology leaders.

What I will remember most, however, is the people.

I met faculty members from across North America who are deeply committed to preparing students for an AI-enabled future. I experienced firsthand the passion and dedication of the SAP University Alliances team, whose educational resources and programs make a real difference in classrooms around the world. And I was welcomed into a community where many members have been collaborating, learning from one another, and building lasting friendships for more than 15 years.

The keynote sessions, customer and partner presentations, and conversations throughout the conference reinforced something I have always believed: the future of innovation depends on strong partnerships between academia and industry.

A special thank you to Evan Sevits and the entire SAP University Alliances North America team for the invitation, the opportunity to present, and for organizing such an outstanding event.

I leave this conference inspired, with new friendships, new ideas, and an even stronger commitment to supporting our academic community and helping students connect classroom learning with real-world impact.

Thank you for making a first-time attendee feel like part of the family.

entry.title: Re: Diagnostic Tool (Employee Data Changes)
entry.content: This is really an eye-opening discussion supported by practical examples.
